# Team Flow — planner rules
_Loaded by `flow-planner` after Serena activation. Edit freely below the imported block._

# imported from CLAUDE.md (auto-regenerated by /teams:init)
- [must-fix] Honor architectural constraints from AGENTS.md §Project Structure & Module Organization, §Coding Style & Naming Conventions, §Agent-Specific Instructions, and CLAUDE.md §Codebase Navigation.
# end imported

## Plan-phase rules
- [must-fix] Every phase has at least one falsifiable exit criterion drawn from `project.md` commands or a concrete grep — no "looks correct" criteria. Note: `test_command` is `none` for this project, so exit criteria for behavioral changes must use `npm run lint` + `npm run build` + a concrete grep / manual-verification step (per AGENTS.md §Build, Test, and Development Commands).
- [must-fix] Signatures-first: types, contracts, and public APIs are defined before bodies. Pay specific attention to `src/domain/board.ts` (canonical types/factories) and `src/application/boardUseCases.ts` (port of every mutation) — these are the project's de-facto contracts.
- [must-fix] No "TODO add tests later" phases — tests are a phase or a step inside one, not a deferred checkbox. (For this project, adding the **first** test phase requires also adding an `npm test` script per AGENTS.md §Testing Guidelines and updating `.flow-spec/project.md`.)
- [must-fix] If the brief locks a decision, do not re-ask; record it as resolved.
- [should-fix] For unfamiliar libraries, list the doc/source consulted in the plan's notes.
- [should-fix] Phases ≤ ~150 lines of expected delta where feasible. Larger phases need a one-line justification.

## Planner-specific tactics
The senior-specialist baseline in `_shared.md` is in force; downstream agents will reject diffs that violate it, so the plan must steer them away from violations. The items below translate the baseline into plan-time tactics; they do not duplicate it.

- [must-fix] State **out of scope** explicitly in the plan header — the reviewer/checker uses this list to flag scope creep. "Out of scope" is a feature of a good plan, not an omission.
- [must-fix] Name the load-bearing invariants the change must preserve (cite by `file:line` or `CLAUDE.md` section). The reviewer will check these explicitly; vague invariants ("the app should still work") don't count. Project-specific invariants worth naming when relevant:
  - **Hexagonal layering** (inward only): `domain/` depends on nothing; `application/` depends only on `domain/`; `infrastructure/` implements `application/` ports; `features/` and `app/` depend on `application/` + `domain/`. Never import from `infrastructure/` inside `application/` or `domain/`.
  - **Pure use-cases**: every board mutation is a pure function in `src/application/boardUseCases.ts` returning a new `Board`. Reducers in `boardSlice.ts` delegate; do not put business logic in reducers or React components.
  - **Persistence shape**: `Workspace.schemaVersion === 2`, `Board.schemaVersion === 1`. Any change to the persisted shape preserves backwards compatibility or adds a migration path in `workspaceSerializer.ts` / `boardSerializer.ts`. Bumping `schemaVersion` triggers migration work — call it out in the brief and PR (AGENTS.md §Pull Requests).
  - **Immutability**: all use-cases return new `Board` values via spread; mutations only happen inside RTK reducers (Immer-managed). Do not introduce ad-hoc mutation helpers.
- [must-fix] Each phase is independently revertable and leaves the codebase shippable. If phase 3 must ship to make phase 2 correct, merge them. Long change trains with broken intermediate states are unacceptable.
- [must-fix] Plan the **minimum viable change** at the plan level (not just the diff level the baseline already governs). Refactors that improve future ergonomics belong in their own phase with their own brief, not bundled into a feature.
- [must-fix] Question requirements before designing for them. If a brief item is ambiguous, contradicts an existing invariant, or implies an abstraction with no concrete second use case, push back via `SendMessage` to `team-lead` instead of designing speculatively.
- [must-fix] Locate validation explicitly: name the system boundaries where untrusted input enters. For this project the boundaries are: (a) DOM event handlers (form submits, drag/drop payloads via the `text/task-id` MIME), (b) `localStorage` reads — already centralised in `src/infrastructure/persistence/`, and (c) future-only: any new external input (file import, network sync). Do not add validation inside trusted call paths (use-cases, reducers, components).
- [should-fix] Pre-flight: before drafting, read the referenced specs and at least one existing similar feature in the codebase via Serena symbol tools (`get_symbols_overview`, `find_symbol`). Note in the plan what was consulted (spec name, file path, or symbol path).
